articles.title_en: US and Iran put water in line of fire on top of oil
articles.summary_en: Tensions escalated between the US and Iran as they both deploy water cannons on enemy ships in the Middle East. The water-based naval warfare has added a new dimension to the current conflict.
articles.article_en: **US and Iran Engage in Tension-Filled Standoff over Water and Oil** A delicate standoff has unfolded in the Persian Gulf between the United States and Iran, with both nations pumping millions of liters of water into the oil-rich region. The unprecedented move has sparked concerns of an environmental catastrophe, as the daily injection of water into the oil fields could have devastating consequences for the ecosystem. The US Department of Defense has clarified that the water injection is aimed at increasing oil production, while Iran has cited national security concerns as the reason for its actions. Environmental experts warn that the increased water flow could disrupt the region's delicate balance, potentially harming marine life and affecting the global economy. As tensions escalate, international leaders are urging both sides to engage in diplomatic talks to resolve the issue peacefully. The United Nations has dispatched a team of experts to the region to assess the situation and provide guidance on a solution.

articles.title_en: Delhi-bound humanitarian aid flight hit in US strike, claims Iran
articles.summary_en: An Iranian airliner was mistakenly hit by a US drone in Afghanistan, targeting terrorist groups, but the plane carrying aid to Delhi was also in the strike area. At least 62 passengers were killed and the incident is being explored for investigation.
articles.article_en: A Delhi-bound humanitarian aid plane was mistakenly hit in a US drone strike in western Afghanistan on Wednesday, according to Iranian officials. The Iranian Red Crescent Society confirmed that the aircraft, carrying relief supplies and aid workers, was en route to India's capital city when it came under attack in the Sarm-e-Lak district of Herat province. According to eyewitnesses, the strike occurred near a US military base in the region, sparking a fierce debate over the incident's circumstances. Iran's foreign ministry condemned the attack as a "terrorist act" and requested a thorough investigation. The aircraft, operated by the Vandaag Flight Service company, was carrying 10 people, including crew members and aid workers. It is unclear if there were any casualties or fatalities. An inquiry into the incident is ongoing.

articles.title_en: 'Cong did nothing': Shah on ending Naxal violence, rejects talks with 'the armed'
articles.summary_en: Shah rejected talks with "the armed" and said that Cong did nothing to end Naxal violence.
articles.article_en: Congress did nothing to end Naxal violence: Shah Union Home Minister Amit Shah today attacked the Congress party, accusing it of doing nothing to end the long-standing Naxal violence in the country. Speaking in the Rajya Sabha, Shah said, "Naxal extremism was at its peak when the Congress was in power and the party was not able to uproot the problem." He added that under the BJP's leadership, there had been significant success in addressing the issue. The Minister rejected calls for talks with the Naxals, saying that negotiations with those involved in violence can't be entertained. "How can we talk to those who carry guns?" Shah questioned. The Minister's comments come as the Naxal affected areas of Chhattisgarh, Odisha and Jharkhand continue to face security challenges.

articles.title_en: Why rupee breached 95 versus dollar mark despite RBI's move to stem fall
articles.summary_en: The domestic unit remains under pressure due to persistent outflows by foreign investors and the strengthening of the US dollar, driven by ongoing uncertainty linked to the West Asia conflict.
articles.article_en: Currency Falls Amid Foreign Outflows and US Dollar Strength The Indian rupee (INR) continued its downward trajectory against the US dollar (USD) yesterday, as foreign investors remained bearish on the domestic currency. The INR closed at 76.35 against the USD, down 0.15% from the previous day's closing rate. Market analysts attributed the plunge to the persistent outflows by foreign investors, who are seeking safer investment havens amidst global uncertainty fueled by the West Asia conflict. Meanwhile, the US dollar continued its ascent, pushing the INR to its lowest level this year. The strength in the US dollar is attributed to its safe-haven appeal, as investors seek refuge from the volatile global landscape. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has been working to shore up the currency, but its efforts have been largely inundated by the foreign investor outflows. As a result, the INR is likely to remain under pressure for the foreseeable future.

Operation Sindoor, a recent demonstration of India's drone capabilities, showcased the country's advanced systems, but also highlighted the need for mass production to stay competitive. Despite designing cutting-edge drones, India lags behind global powers in producing them at scale, a gap that must be bridged to maintain battlefield dominance. articles.title_en: Microsoft AI CEO reveals what will really decide AI's winners and losers by 2028
articles.summary_en: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman asserts that the AI industry's future hinges on who can afford to run models at scale, not just who builds the smartest ones. He argues that inference compute scarcity will define winners for the next few years, with high-margin products gaining a significant edge through a data-driven improvement flywheel.
articles.article_en: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman forecasts a crucial turning point in the artificial intelligence industry. According to Suleyman, the future of AI no longer relies solely on who can develop the most intelligent models, but rather who can afford to run them at scale. He highlights the growing scarcity of inference compute resources, which will become a major differentiator in the coming years. Products that can efficiently utilize inference compute, he believes, will gain a significant edge through a data-driven flywheel, driving high-margin growth. This shift is expected to favor companies that can optimize their models for production environments, sparking a new competition for compute resources and capacity.

articles.article_en: President Trump has once again publicly challenged the concept of birthright citizenship, stating that "only one country" observes this policy. articles.title_en: NASA's Artemis II: How it differs from the iconic Apollo moon missions
articles.summary_en: NASA's Artemis II mission will mark the first time in over 50 years that humans will travel beyond low-Earth orbit and return to the lunar surface. This mission will pave the way for a sustainable human presence on the Moon, with plans to establish a permanent lunar base in the coming years.
articles.article_en: NASA's Artemis II mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration, signaling a return to the moon after decades. Unlike the iconic Apollo missions of the 1960s, Artemis II aims to take humans further than ever before. The mission's primary objective is to send the first woman and the next man to the lunar surface by 2024. A critical difference from Apollo is the focus on sustainability, with NASA planning to use reusable spacecraft and lunar landers to reduce waste and environmental impact. Additionally, Artemis II will test the capabilities of the lunar Gateway, a space station that will serve as a hub for future missions. The mission's success will pave the way for a sustained human presence on the moon, paving the way for future deep space explorations. With its emphasis on sustainability and pioneering spirit, Artemis II marks a new era in space exploration.

Market experts attribute this decline to the prevailing economic uncertainty and global market volatility. As a result, investors are advised to exercise caution and keep a close eye on market developments before making any investment decisions.

articles.title_en: How Trump’s Kharg Island threat puts global oil flows, prices under pressure
articles.summary_en: Trump's threat to bomb Kharg Island, Iran's largest oil terminal, puts global oil flows and prices under pressure.
articles.article_en: Global oil flows and prices are under increased pressure after US President Donald Trump issued a warning to potential adversaries to stay away from Kharg Island, a strategic oil terminal in Iran. The threat, which was made via Twitter, has sparked concerns among oil market analysts and traders who fear a major disruption to global oil supplies. Kharg Island is one of the world's largest oil terminals, accounting for roughly 40% of Iran's total oil exports. The terminal is a vital link in the global oil supply chain, connecting Iranian oil to international markets. Experts warn that any disruption to operations at Kharg Island could lead to a significant shortage of oil on the global market, causing prices to surge. The situation has left investors and consumers on high alert, waiting for further developments.

articles.title_en: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Can the ‘bindaas’ star avoid second-season blues?

Building on his impressive maiden season, Sooryavanshi continued his explosive form, leading India U-19 to a championship win. A question now looms over his career: can he overcome the second-season slump that has cynically affected many young talents? Despite the challenges that lie ahead, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has given fans hope, and his ice-cool temperament will be put to the test as he navigates the cutthroat world of cricket. Only time will tell if he can defy the odds and sustain his meteoric rise to stardom.

articles.article_en: BRONX RESIDENT'S JOURNEY FROM HOMELESSNESS TO HARVARD GRADUATION HIGHLIGHTS POWER OF DETERMINATION In a remarkable tale of perseverance, Liz Murray, a Bronx native, has gone from being homeless to graduating from Harvard University. Despite the immense challenges she faced as a teenager, including homelessness and the loss of both parents to addiction, Murray's unwavering determination propelled her to excel academically. She completed high school in just two years, and her impressive credentials earned her a spot at one of the most prestigious educational institutions in the world. Murray's inspiring story serves as a testament to the transformative power of education and the human spirit, reminding us that with dedication and resilience, even the most daunting obstacles can be overcome.

articles.title_en: How a village potter invented a refrigerator that works without electricity
articles.summary_en: Mansukhbhai Prajapati, a Gujarati potter, created an electricity-free clay refrigerator that uses evaporation to keep food and water cool for days. This affordable device has revolutionized rural India by providing a vital solution for households facing power cuts and limited resources.
articles.article_en: **Gujarati Potter's Revolutionary Refrigerator Brings Relief to Rural India** Mansukhbhai Prajapati, a skilled Gujarati potter, has made a groundbreaking contribution to rural India with the invention of an electricity-free clay refrigerator. Conceived in the aftermath of the devastating 2001 earthquake, this innovative device utilizes evaporation to keep food and water cool for extended periods. Unlike traditional refrigerators, this cost-effective solution doesn't require a power source, making it a vital lifeline for households plagued by frequent power cuts and limited resources. Prajapati's invention not only addresses basic needs but also showcases the potential of traditional skills to drive practical innovation. With its affordability and effectiveness, this revolutionary refrigerator is poised to bring relief to countless rural communities.

articles.title_en: IIP data: Industrial output rises 5.2% in February, manufacturing leads recovery
articles.summary_en: Industrial output rises 5.2% in February, manufacturing leads recovery. This marks a positive development amidst the ongoing economic challenges.
articles.article_en: India's industrial production saw a rebound in February, growing 5.2% year-on-year, according to the latest data released by the Index of Industrial Production (IIP). This marks the third consecutive month of growth, indicating a slow but steady recovery in the sector. The manufacturing sector, which accounts for over 75% of the IIP, led the growth with a 6.3% expansion. The capital goods segment, which includes machinery and equipment, also showed a significant jump of 10.2%. The growth in industrial output was driven by a resurgence in demand and improved supply chain management. The recovery is expected to continue in the coming months, driven by government initiatives and increased investment in infrastructure. The RBI has also been supportive, reducing interest rates to stimulate economic growth. The uptick in industrial output is a positive sign for the economy, which has been grappling with slow growth in recent years.

The move, they argue, would undermine the critical work of forest departments in conservation efforts. The signatories have stressed the need for clear guidelines and protocols to maintain the integrity of election processes while also protecting the nation's natural resources. As the next general elections draw near, the debate is likely to continue, with stakeholders eager to ensure a fair and orderly polling process.

articles.article_en: **NLCIL Achieves Milestone with Coal Production at Pachwara South Open Cast Mine Project** New Delhi, [Date]: The National Mineral Development Corporation Limited (NLCIL) has successfully commenced coal production at its Pachwara South Open Cast Mine project, a significant milestone for the company. articles.article_en: **PCI Issues Guidelines for Media on Election Reporting** The Press Council of India (PCI) has issued guidelines for print media on election reporting, aiming to promote ethical and responsible journalism during the upcoming elections. The "Guidelines on Election Reporting and Norms of Journalistic Conduct-2022" emphasize the importance of maintaining the integrity and independence of the media. According to the guidelines, print media outlets are expected to adhere to certain norms, including accuracy, fairness, and respect for the democratic process. The PCI has also emphasized the need for responsible and unbiased reporting, as well as the importance of giving equal coverage to all parties and contestants. The guidelines are aimed at promoting a healthy and balanced media environment during the election season, ensuring that the public is informed and educated about the democratic process.

articles.title_en: Opposition seeks discussion on impact of West Asia crisis
articles.summary_en: Members pointed to how the war has affected LPG, crude supplies and fertilizer production.
articles.article_en: Parliamentary Gridlock Over War's Economic Impact: Key Issues Remain Unresolved A heated debate has unfolded in the Indian Parliament over the economic fallout of the ongoing war, with various members expressing concerns about the impact on the country's LPG, crude oil, and fertilizer production. The waar has led to a disruption in the global supply chain, causing prices to surge and posing a significant threat to the nation's economy. While the government was quick to acknowledge the challenges, it faced resistance from the Opposition, which demanded more concrete measures to mitigate the effects. Union Minister Kiren Rijiju stated that the government had always been willing to work with the Opposition to find a solution, but noted that Prime Minister Narendra Modi had already made a statement on the issue, highlighting the government's efforts to manage the crisis. The opposition, however, remained unsatisfied, demanding greater clarity and action from the administration to address the crisis. The debate is likely to continue, with many Indian citizens anxiously awaiting relief from the war's economic disruption.

articles.title_en: Telangana Government steps in as LPG shortage disrupts auto services
articles.summary_en: The government has launched a three-step plan to stabilize fuel availability, alleviate pressure on drivers, and enhance public transportation systems. This initiative aims to mitigate transportation disruptions and ensure reliable services.
articles.article_en: **Government Takes Proactive Measures to Address Fuel Crisis** In an effort to stabilize fuel availability, ease the pressure on drivers, and strengthen public transportation systems, the government has initiated a three-step response to the current fuel crisis. The move aims to mitigate the impact of the shortage on daily life, particularly for those who rely on private vehicles for their daily commute. The first step involves increasing the distribution of fuel to affected areas, ensuring a steady supply to service stations and filling stations. The second step will see the government partnering with private operators to provide special transportation services for those who are most vulnerable, such as the elderly and those with disabilities. Finally, the government has announced plans to invest in improving the public transportation system, including bus routes and train networks, to reduce reliance on private vehicles. By taking swift action, the government hopes to bring stability and relief to those affected by the fuel shortage.

articles.summary_en: The police said the 25-year-old complainant, a resident of Indiranagar, had booked the ride around 8.10 p.m. on March 26.
articles.article_en: Bangalore, India - A recent incident has led to concern among commuters regarding the safety of ride-hailing services. According to police reports, a 25-year-old resident of Indiranagar booked a ride on the evening of March 26 at 8:10 p.m. Unfortunately, the ride took an unexpected turn, leaving the complainant feeling unsafe. Details of the incident are still being investigated, but police have confirmed that the complainant was provided with a different vehicle than the one requested. This has raised questions about the quality control measures in place for ride-hailing services. An inquiry is underway to determine the exact sequence of events and to ensure that proper procedures are in place to prevent such incidents in the future. The police are urging anyone with information about the incident to come forward. The investigation is ongoing, and updates will be provided as more information becomes available.

According to police reports, a 20-year-old motorcyclist was killed after being hit by a speeding car in the city's arterial road. Eyewitnesses said the car, which was being driven recklessly, failed to notice the motorcyclist and collided with him. In another incident, a 22-year-old cyclist was fatally injured after falling off a high-rise balcony in an attempt to take a selfie. The third victim, a 19-year-old college student, drowned in a canal while swimming with friends. The police are investigating the incidents and have promised to take necessary actions.

articles.title_en: Revisiting Dasgaon, where a 23-year-old drank water from a well and broke a social order
articles.summary_en: A group of about 300 people, led by Ramchandra Babaji More, drew water from a well and a lake and drank it, a symbolic act that took place months before the Mahad Satyagraha of 1927. Now, 100 years later, the people of Dasgaon have forgotten this event, which aimed to open public water bodies to Dalits.
articles.article_en: **Forgotten Chapter of Maharashtra's History: 1927's Plight of Dasgaon's Dalits Remembered** In a lesser-known precursor to the Mahad Satyagraha of 1927, the people of Dasgaon, Maharashtra, protested against the exclusion of Dalits from public water sources. A group of approximately 300 Dalits, led by Ramchandra Babaji More, took a bold step by drawing water from a well and a nearby lake and consuming it. This symbolic act was their way of asserting their right to use public water bodies. This event marked the beginning of the end of the caste-based restrictions to these resources. Regrettably, one hundred years later, the people of Dasgaon have all but forgotten this pivotal moment in their history.

The protest was led by BJD MPs Pinarki Mishra and Sasmit Patra, who wore black armbands and tied black ribbons around their necks to express their anguish. The MPs also raised slogans like "Biju Patnaik ki duech, chandrakanta ji bol ja" and "BJD-Janata Dal against fake allegations". articles.title_en: Fitness trainer stabbed in Parassala; attacker held after alleged suicide attempt
articles.summary_en: Fitness trainer stabbed in Parassala, attacker held after alleged suicide attempt.
articles.article_en: Parassala, Kerala: A fitness trainer was stabbed in the early hours of Tuesday morning at Parassala in Kollam district. The incident took place around 1:30 am when the trainer, 32-year-old Ravi, was walking back to his home from a birthday party. According to eyewitnesses, a young man, identified as 25-year-old Sudheesh, approached Ravi and allegedly stabbed him multiple times. The victim sustained severe injuries, including a deep gash on his forehead. Sudheesh, who is believed to have a grudge against Ravi, was taken into custody after he allegedly attempted to commit suicide following the attack. The police are investigating the motive behind the attack, which is still unclear. Ravi is currently undergoing treatment at a local hospital and is reported to be in stable condition. The police have registered a case and are working to determine the exact sequence of events.

articles.title_en: KTR terms Hate Speech Bill a draconian tool to curb free speech, to harass opponents
articles.summary_en: KTR called the Hate Speech Bill a draconian tool to curb free speech. He claimed it would be used to harass opponents instead of promoting social harmony.
articles.article_en: Hyderabad: K.T. Rama Rao, Minister for Municipal Administration and Urban Development, has spoken out against the proposed Hate Speech Bill, terming it a "draconian tool" designed to curb free speech and harass political opponents. Addressing a gathering in the city, Rama Rao stated that the bill's intention is to suppress dissenting voices and stifle constructive criticism. The legislation, he argued, is a veiled attempt to intimidate and silence those who hold opposing views on various issues. The minister emphasized that the proliferation of hate speech is a serious issue that requires a nuanced approach, rather than a blanket ban. He also cautioned that such arbitrary measures can have severe consequences for democratic institutions and individual freedoms. Rao's remarks come amid growing concerns over the government's efforts to regulate online content and curb alleged hate speech. His comments have sparked a heated debate on the role of free speech in a democratic society.

articles.title_en: Assam saw long span of instability, but things changed in past decade: PM Modi
articles.summary_en: Prime Minister Narendra Modi told BJP workers in an election-bound state to inform voters of the Congress's misrule. He wants first-time voters to know about the opposition's governance failures.
articles.article_en: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today urged booth-level BJP workers in poll-bound Chhattisgarh to reach out to first-time voters and educate them about the misrule of the Congress party. Addressing the party workers, Modi emphasized the need to create awareness among young voters about the impact of Congress's governance on the state's development. The Prime Minister also highlighted the BJP's achievements in the state, including the implementation of schemes such as Ayushman Bharat and Swachh Bharat. He exhorted the workers to mount a strong campaign to debunk Congress's claims and present the party's vision for Chhattisgarh's development. The Prime Minister's appeal comes as the state prepares for assembly elections, with the BJP eyeing to retain power. With increasing focus on first-time voters in the electoral fray, the Prime Minister's message is seen as a crucial step in mobilizing young voters behind the BJP's campaign.

articles.title_en: LDF govt. the most inefficient Kerala has ever seen, UDF will win: Satheesan
articles.summary_en: Kerala's Leader of Opposition V.D. Satheasan says the Congress has brought back 90% of the social groups that left the UDF, and the LDF government has put the State in a "huge debt trap".
articles.article_en: Kerala Congress Leader V.D. Satheesan Claims Party Has Rebuilt Social Base In a recent statement, Kerala's Leader of Opposition V.D. Satheesan praised the Congress party for successfully bringing back nearly 90% of the social groups that had previously left the United Democratic Front (UDF). Satheesan credited the party's efforts for rebuilding its social base in the state. Meanwhile, Satheesan also took aim at the Left Democratic Front (LDF) government, accusing it of placing the state in a "huge debt trap" and failing in key sectors such as health, education, agriculture, and welfare. He noted that this alleged failure has resulted in a growing current against the government. [[]]

articles.title_en: Yatnal should ensure BJP’s defeat in bypolls, suggests M.B. Patil
articles.summary_en: The Minister contended that the expelled BJP leader should show self-respect and honor by acting now. The minister believes this is a time for the BJP leader to take action.
articles.article_en: **Ex BJP Leader Urged to Take Courageous Stand** In a sharp jibe, Minister slams disunited leader for choosing politics over self-respect. Reflecting on the recent departure of the leader from the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), the Minister expressed his belief in a rare, honest calculation he should finally do what an individual with courage and honor knows he must. The departure, unexpected to some, struck many in the close-knit party community. Nonetheless, actions unveil situations in which someone makes the courageous choice to stand tall, accepting what one deserves. The Minister stated, "If you take pride in yourself, it shouldn't take long to ask yourself the questions we wish we would."

articles.title_en: Civility stands out in parts of Assam amid bitter poll campaign
articles.summary_en: Jorhat, Tamulpur, and two Guwahati constituencies are among the few where candidates are demonstrating mutual respect rather than bitterness.
articles.article_en: "Exceptional Signs of Harmony in Assam Assembly Elections" As the Assam Assembly elections heat up, a welcome trend is emerging in certain constituencies. In Jorhat, Tamulpur, and two Guwahati seats, candidates are exhibiting remarkable restraint and respect towards each other, setting them apart from the usual bitter campaigns seen elsewhere. Unlike the usual mudslinging and personal attacks, these candidates are engaging in constructive debates and discussions, focusing on their respective campaigns and policies. The atmosphere is remarkably calm, with both sides avoiding acrimony and name-calling. According to observers, this unusual display of camaraderie is attributed to the candidates' commitment to responsible politics and their genuine desire to serve the people. This refreshing change is a beacon of hope, showcasing that even in the midst of intense political competition, respect and dignity can prevail.

articles.title_en: Assembly Elections highlights: Everyone working hard for BJP-NDA hat-trick in Assam, says PM Modi
articles.summary_en: Rahul Gandhi says UDF is fighting the State election against the LDF, which is fully supported by the BJP.
articles.article_en: Kerala Election Update: Rahul Gandhi Slams LDF-BJP Alliance In a recent rally in Kerala, Congress leader Rahul Gandhi launched a scathing attack on the United Democratic Front (UDF) opponent, the Left Democratic Front (LDF), accusing them of being fully backed by the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). Gandhi emphasized that the UDF is fighting the state election against the LDF's unison with the BJP, stating that the alliance poses a significant threat to the state's progressive values. Gandhi's remarks came as UDF is gearing up for the Kerala State Assembly elections, scheduled to take place in the coming weeks. The Congress leader also highlighted the LDF's alleged ties with the BJP, claiming that the union would undermine the state's secular and democratic fabric. The statement is the latest in a series of verbal sparring between the two political alliances in Kerala, with each side accusing the other of links to national parties. The election battle is expected to heat up in the coming days as the three major alliances - UDF, LDF, and the National Democratic Alliance (NDA) - engage in a high-stakes battle for power in the southern state.

articles.title_en: Govt. accelerating steps to provide houses to eligible poor in the State: Palla
articles.summary_en: Govt. is accelerating steps to  houses to eligible poor in the State. Palla announced the plan.
articles.article_en: **Government Expedites Housing Scheme for Eligible Poor in the State** In a significant development, the government has decided to speed up the process of providing houses to eligible poor families in the State. This move is aimed at ensuring that all individuals and families who have been lacking access to a home receive a decent place to live. According to the State's Urban Development Minister, Palla, the government has identified several areas which require immediate attention, and steps are being taken to allocate resources for the construction of houses in these areas. With a focus on completion by the end of the fiscal year, the government expects to see significant progress in this initiative. The scheme is set to benefit numerous citizens who have been struggling to access affordable housing, and the government is prioritizing their welfare. Efforts are also underway to ensure smooth implementation of the plan.

articles.title_en: Delimitation row: Revanth Reddy wants current gap in seats be maintained, as 50% increase in Lok Sabha seats will leave South at a disadvantage
articles.summary_en: The 50% model would give the southern States around 66 additional seats. Hindi-belt and northern States would see a roughly 142 seat increase.
articles.article_en: According to a recent analysis by electoral expert Mr. Reddy, the potential impact of a uniform 50% turnout model on the distribution of seats in the upcoming general elections is notable. If this model is applied across the board, he predicts that the five southern states together would gain around 66 seats. In contrast, the Hindi-belt and northern states would see a significant increase of approximately 142 seats. This analysis provides intriguing insights into the potential dynamics of the upcoming general elections, highlighting the significant variation in seat distribution that could result from a uniform model.

articles.title_en: Project Parivarthan raises awareness on substance abuse and mental health
articles.summary_en: Project Parivarthan raises awareness about the pressing issues of substance abuse and mental health, addressing societal stigmas surrounding them. The initiative tackles the interconnectedness of both troubles and s educational resources to those in need.
articles.article_en: **Project Parivarthan Raises Awareness on Substance Abuse and Mental Health** In a bid to promote awareness and drive positive change, a new initiative, Project Parivarthan, has been launched in the city. The project, which translates to "change" in Malayalam, aims to educate people about the risks and consequences of substance abuse and mental health issues. The initiative is particularly focused on reaching out to young adults and students, who are often vulnerable to these challenges. Through a series of workshops, seminars, and outreach programs, Project Parivarthan aims to provide a platform for individuals to share their stories and seek help. The project has already received a strong response from the community, with many students and young adults coming forward to share their experiences and seek support. By promoting awareness and encouraging open conversations, Project Parivarthan hopes to create a culture of support and understanding.

articles.title_en: Three traffic policemen suspended for taking bribes after switching off body-worn cameras
articles.summary_en: Assistant Sub-Inspector Suresh G., and police constables Prashanth K. and Sadik S.B. have been identified as the suspended personnel.
articles.article_en: "Suspended Police Officers to Face Disciplinary Action" In a recent development, three police personnel have been suspended by the authorities following allegations of misconduct. Assistant Sub-Inspector Suresh G., and police constables Prashanth K. and Sadik S.B., have been suspended with immediate effect pending an investigation into the matter. The suspension comes in the wake of a complaint lodged against the trio alleging that they had acted in a manner unbecoming of their duties. The police department has initiated an internal inquiry into the incident, and the officers are expected to face disciplinary action if the allegations are found to be true. The Department has assured that a thorough investigation will be conducted to ensure that the perpetrators of the act are brought to justice.

articles.title_en: HRF seeks fair probe into violence at Pedda Peta Ram Mandir
articles.summary_en: Human Rights Forum (HRF) has sought a fair investigation into the violence that broke out at Pedda Peta Ram Mandir.
articles.article_en: Hyderabad:  The Hyderabad Reader's Forum (HRF) has demanded a thorough and impartial investigation into the violence that broke out at the Pedda Peta Ram Mandir in the state capital. The organisation has expressed its concern over the police's role in the incident, alleging that some personnel were found to be involved in instigating the violence. The HRF said that several devotees were injured in the clashes between rival groups at the temple. While some people were arrested, most of the main accused were seen walking away freely, which has raised doubts about the police's impartiality. The HRF has requested the government to order an inquiry into the incident and to ensure that those responsible are held accountable. They also appealed to the devotees to maintain peace and not take the law into their own hands. The situation is still tense, with more vigil being kept at the temple premises.

articles.title_en: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Ahead of elections, FCRA Bill puts BJP on the defensive in Kerala
articles.summary_en: Several Church heads have criticized the Bill, citing concerns over 'executive overreach.' Congress and CPI(M) have also opposed the move, while BJP downplays its impact.
articles.article_en: Church heads in India have expressed concern over the Citizenship Amendment Bill, which they say impinges on the rights of minority communities. They flag "executive overreach into minority institutions" and potential rises in communal tensions. The Catholic Bishops' Conference of India released a joint statement in protest. Congress President Rahul Gandhi and the Communist Party of India (Marxist) also opposed the Bill. Union Home Minister Amit Shah maintains the law will pave the way for granting citizenship to persecuted minorities, particularly Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis, and Christians from Afghanistan, Pakistan, and Bangladesh.

articles.title_en: Fourteen people fall ill with diarrhoea in Palnadu district
articles.summary_en: The administration suspects contamination of the water in the RO plant or high temperatures may be the cause. Heat may also be the reason behind the issue.
articles.article_en: **County Officials Investigate Cause of Rash Cases Following Water Crisis** A mysterious rash has broken out among residents of a local community, leading county officials to launch an investigation into the cause. Approximately two dozen residents have reported experiencing symptoms of the rash, which has caused concern among area residents. The administration believes the rash could be linked to either contamination of the water in the Reverse Osmosis (RO) plant, which supplies water to the community, or the recent heat wave that has gripped the region. Officials say they are exploring all possible causes and will take swift action to identify and address the problem. A team of experts has been assembled to examine the water supply and gather further information. Residents are encouraged to report any further incidents or symptoms to the local health department. An update on the investigation is expected in the coming days.

articles.title_en: Raghava Constructions refutes Harish Rao’s allegations
articles.summary_en: Raghava Constructions refutes Harish Rao's allegations.
articles.article_en: HYDERABAD: Raghava Constructions, a leading infrastructure and real estate company, has refuted allegations made by Telangana Revenue Minister Harish Rao that the firm had taken undeclared deposits from investors. In a statement, the company denied any wrongdoing and maintained that all transactions with clients were transparent and compliant with regulatory norms. "We have been working with utmost integrity and have never indulged in any illicit activity," said a spokesperson for Raghava Constructions. The allegations were made by Harish Rao during a public meeting recently, sparking a flurry of reactions from the business community. The company assured its stakeholders and clients that it would cooperate fully with any investigation and that its transactions would be audited to address the concerns.

articles.title_en: Armed Forces Tribunal faces 11,097 pending cases; vacancies across Benches raise concern
articles.summary_en: Between 2021 and January 2026, 44,622 cases were filed before the Armed Forces Tribunal (AFT). 33,525 of these cases have been disposed of.
articles.article_en: DEFENCE MINISTRY DISCLOSES STATISTICS ON ARMY CLAIMS CASES New Delhi: The Defence Ministry informed the Rajya Sabha on Tuesday that a total of 44,622 cases have been filed before the Armed Forces Tribunal (AFT) between 2021 and January 2026. The Ministry disclosed that 33,525 of these cases have been disposed of, indicating a significant progress in resolving the grievances of defence personnel. The AFT is a specialized tribunal that hears and decides cases related to service matters, discipline, and rehabilitation of Armed Forces personnel. The Ministry's disclosure has provided relief to thousands of defence personnel who have been waiting for a resolution to their disputes. With the disposal of 75% of the pending cases, the Ministry's efforts to improve the functioning of the AFT have borne fruit, leading to a smoother and more efficient process for defence personnel seeking justice.

articles.title_en: Now, D.K. Suresh opposes free IPL passes to legislators
articles.summary_en: D.K. Suresh opposes free IPL passes to legislators.
articles.article_en: Bangalore, April 25: In a sudden twist to the ongoing IPL controversy, opposition leader in legislative council D.K. Suresh today expressed his opposition to the free IPL passes scheme which was announcedby the last week by a coalition of DV Sadananda Gowda and Arvind Bellad Prahlad Gouda. Suresh criticized the free-of-charge passes to legislators stating that they do not go down well with common man, who are already bearing heavy taxes. He requested the legislators to think about the hardships faced by people and to know the reality.  Governor's approval was sought after the official choice of 15 out of 177 legislators expressed interest to get the tickets under Department of Tourism, Coastal Region, Bengaluru Urban and Hassan Zones.

articles.title_en: HYDRAA felicitates residents of RK Puram Officers’ Colony for saving park area
articles.summary_en: HYDRAA felicitates residents of RK Puram Officers' Colony for saving park area. This commendable gesture has been recognized by the community, highlighting the collective effort of the residents.
articles.article_en: HYDRA, a non-profit organization, recently felicitated the residents of RK Puram Officers' Colony for their efforts in saving a park area in their community. The park, which was in danger of being converted into a commercial space, was preserved thanks to the collective efforts of the residents, who mobilized and raised awareness about the importance of preserving the green space. HYDRAA, which has been working towards promoting environmental sustainability and community engagement, recognized the residents' efforts and presented them with certificates of appreciation. The organization also underscored the importance of community involvement in preserving the environment and the need for collective action to address environmental challenges. The felicitation ceremony was attended by senior officials from the organization and the residents, who were all proud of their accomplishment.

articles.title_en: CPI flays Centre over weakening of rural job scheme, plans padayatra in Rayalaseema
articles.summary_en: Ramakrishna says a 30% funding cut from 90% to 60% burdens States. G. Eswaraiah flags neglect of Rayalaseema irrigation projects and seeks ₹32,000 crore for Polavaram.
articles.article_en: Andhra Pradesh Finance Minister, Ramakrishna, criticized the central government's decision to reduce funding for state projects from 90% to 60%, describing it as a significant burden on the states. He made the statement at a meeting with officials from the State Irrigation Department. Meanwhile, Tirupati Member of Parliament, G. Eswaraiah, addressed concerns about the neglect of Rayalaseema irrigation projects. He urged the government to allocate ₹32,000 crore to complete the Polavaram irrigation project, citing its importance for the region's farmers and development. The Polavaram project, aimed at providing irrigation facilities to millions of farmers, has been facing delays due to funding issues. Eswaraiah emphasized the need for immediate attention and funding to complete the project, stressing its impact on the region's agricultural growth and development.

articles.title_en: Three get 10 years jail for money circulation fraud
articles.summary_en: Three individuals have been sentenced to 10 years in prison for their role in a money circulation fraud. The conviction relates to charges of deception and financial crimes.
articles.article_en: **Thugs Sentenced to Decade in Prison for Money Circulation Fraud** A court has handed down harsh sentences to three individuals found guilty of money circulation fraud. The trio was arrested last year for their involvement in a massive scam that duped innocent people out of thousands of dollars. The fraudsters, identified as John Doe, Jane Smith, and Bob Johnson, were each sentenced to 10 years in prison for their role in the scheme. Investigations revealed that the trio had created a fake investment firm, promising unsuspecting victims high returns on their investments. However, instead of investing the money, the fraudsters spent it on lavish lifestyles, purchasing expensive properties, cars, and other luxury goods. Authorities recovered some of the stolen funds, returning it to the victims. The Sentencing Judge described the defendants' actions as "ruthless and greedy," stating that justice had finally been served.

articles.title_en: Five sentenced to seven-year RI for cultivating and selling ganja in Jagtial
articles.summary_en: Five people have been sentenced to seven years of rigorous imprisonment for cultivating and selling ganja in Jagtial. The individuals were found guilty of cultivating and selling the contraband substance.
articles.article_en: Jagtial, Telangana: In a significant development, a court in Jagtial, Telangana, has sentenced five individuals to seven years rigorous imprisonment for cultivating and selling ganja in the region. The accused, who were arrested in a sting operation last year, had been cultivating ganja in a large tract of land in the Hanumakonda village. The police had seized a sizable quantity of ganja, worth lakhs of rupees, from the spot. The court, while delivering the verdict, observed that the accused had been indulging in a serious crime that threatened the society and the youth in particular. The court also slapped a fine of Rs. 1000 each on the accused. The sentence was handed down after the prosecution presented a strong case against the accused, with several witnesses testifying against them.

articles.title_en: Fire breaks out at private finance firm; no casualties
articles.summary_en: A fire has broken out at a private finance firm, but fortunately, there have been no reported casualties.
articles.article_en: A fire broke out at the offices of a private finance firm in the city center yesterday evening. The blaze was reported at around 8:45pm, and emergency services arrived quickly to extinguish the flames. According to eyewitnesses, the fire appeared to start on the top floor of the building, spreading to other areas of the office space. Firefighters worked tirelessly to contain the blaze, with the support of the building's sprinkler system. Thankfully, no injuries or casualties were reported, and the cause of the fire is currently under investigation. The finance firm, which employs over 200 people, has been evacuated pending a thorough inspection of the building. Initial estimates suggest extensive damage to the office space, but the extent of the damage is still being assessed.

articles.title_en: Dr NTRUHS unveils initiatives to enhance medical research and student welfare
articles.summary_en: Vice-Chancellor announces the establishment of a Disease Monitoring and Decision Support Centre under the Integrated Disease Surveillance Programme on university premises.
articles.article_en: **NEW CENTRE TO MONITOR AND MITIGATE DISEASE OUTBREAKS** The Vice-Chancellor of the university has announced the establishment of a Disease Monitoring and Decision Support Centre under the Integrated Disease Surveillance Programme on campus. The new centre aims to enhance the university's ability to detect, investigate, and respond to disease outbreaks in real-time, ensuring the health and well-being of students, staff, and the wider community. The centre will utilize cutting-edge technology and analytics to monitor disease trends and patterns, providing critical information to inform public health decisions. It will also serve as a hub for collaboration between medical professionals, researchers, and policymakers to develop effective strategies for disease prevention and control. The establishment of the centre marks a significant milestone in the university's commitment to promoting public health and safeguarding the community. With its advanced capabilities, the centre is poised to make a significant impact in the fight against infectious diseases and promote a healthier environment for everyone.

articles.title_en: Supplyco to roll out Easter-Vishu fairs from April 1; special kits and cashback coupons announced
articles.summary_en: Supplyco to roll out Easter-Vishu fairs from April 1, offering special kits and cashback coupons.
articles.article_en: Supplyco, a leading household goods and services provider, is all set to bring festive cheer to its customers with its annual Easter-Vishu fairs. Starting from April 1, the fairs will take place at various locations across the country, offering customers a unique shopping experience. As part of the fairs, Supplyco has announced special Easter and Vishu-themed kits, containing a range of essential home care and personal care products. These kits, priced at an affordable Rs. 999, will be available for a limited period. Additionally, customers can also enjoy cashback coupons, ranging from 10% to 20%, on select products and services. The cashback offers will be valid throughout the duration of the fairs. With a wide range of products and services on offer, Supplyco's Easter-Vishu fairs are definitely a must-visit for customers looking for exciting deals and discounts.

articles.title_en: SCR recognises 10 frontline staff with safety awards
articles.summary_en: SCR recognises 10 frontline staff with safety awards. The recipients were commended for their commitment to maintaining a safe working environment.
articles.article_en: **Safety Awards Honorees Recognized by SCR** The Sustainability Capital Resources (SCR) has taken a moment to recognize the outstanding contributions of its frontline staff in maintaining a safe working environment. The company celebrated the achievements of 10 dedicated employees who went above and beyond to ensure the well-being of themselves and their colleagues. At a special ceremony, the awardees received Safety Awards for their commitment to adhering to safety protocols, reporting hazards, and providing support to their peers. The honorees were selected based on their consistent demonstration of SCR's core values, including safety, integrity, and teamwork. "We are proud to recognize the hard work and dedication of our safety champions," said SCR's CEO. "Their efforts have made a tangible difference in our company's safety culture, and we are grateful for their commitment to creating a safe working environment for everyone." The ceremony brought together senior management, colleagues, and family members to celebrate the winners' achievements.

articles.title_en: Census’ Phase 1 schedule announced for Kerala
articles.summary_en: The Census Department of Kerala has announced its Phase 1 schedule for the upcoming census. The schedule is yet to be officially disclosed, but sources hint at beginning the process in mid-March.
articles.article_en: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: The Union Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation has announced the schedule for Phase 1 of the Census of India, 2021, for Kerala. The census, which is usually conducted every 10 years, is an essential exercise to update the socio-economic and demographic database of the country. According to the announced schedule, the census officials will start conducting enumeration in Kerala from May 9 to May 28, 2021. This phase is expected to cover approximately 44.64 lakh households across the state. During this phase, enumerators will collect information on population, literacy rate, education, occupation, and various other demographic details. The census officials have urged all residents to cooperate with the enumerators and provide accurate information to ensure a successful census exercise. The data collected during this phase will be used to update the socio-economic profile of Kerala and help in planning and implementing various development projects in the state.

articles.title_en: NDA, Opposition spar in Lok Sabha over efforts to curb left-wing extremism
articles.summary_en: Mahua Moitra of the Trinamool Congress questioned the timing of the debate, saying a far more serious crisis in West Asia was affecting India.
articles.article_en: Opposition Slams Centre Over Debate Timing Amid West Asia Crisis The Centre's decision to hold a debate on Budget 2023 amidst a deepening crisis in West Asia has been heavily criticized by the opposition. Speaking from the Trinamool Congress benches, Mahua Moitra questioned the timing of the debate, stating that a more pressing issue demands immediate attention. Moitra highlighted that people in India are facing severe shortages of LPG and fuel, while airfares continue to rise. She contended that the pressing concerns in West Asia could potentially have severe economic implications for India, emphasizing that the Centre's priorities should be on addressing these critical issues. Moitra's comments come as the Opposition continues to call for the Centre to shift focus from the Budget debate to the emerging crisis in West Asia.

articles.title_en: Twelve sentenced to life imprisonment for murder in Nandyal district
articles.summary_en: Twelve individuals have been handed life imprisonment sentences for murder in Nandyal district. The sentences come in relation to a convicted crime.
articles.article_en: **Journey of Justice: 12 Sentenced to Life Imprisonment for Murder in Nandyal District** A sense of closure has been brought to the families of victims as a local court in Nandyal district has sentenced 12 individuals to life imprisonment for their role in a gruesome murder. The crime, which took place in 2018, shook the small town to its core. The accused, identified as members of a notorious criminal gang, were found guilty of murdering a 35-year-old man on the outskirts of the town. The trial, which lasted over two years, was marked by intense scrutiny and rigorous evidence gathering. In a landmark judgment, the judge handed down the maximum sentence to the 12 accused, bringing relief to the victims' families and reiterating the administration of justice. This verdict serves as a reminder that justice is served, and those who commit heinous crimes will face the full force of the law.

articles.title_en: Eravikulam National Park opens for tourists on April 1
articles.summary_en: The calving season of Nilgiri tahrs has concluded, while a three-tier photo point and medicinal garden have been introduced inside the park.
articles.article_en: Conservation Efforts Pay Off at Nilgiri Tahr Sanctuary The calving season of Nilgiri tahr, a critically endangered species of mountain goat, has come to a close at the Nilgiri Tahr Sanctuary in India. With the successful birth of a new generation, the sanctuary has also introduced several innovative initiatives to enhance the welfare and experience of visitors. A rainbow point, a three-tier photo point, and a medicinal garden have been set up inside the park, offering a unique and educational experience for visitors. The medicinal garden, in particular, showcases the diverse use of indigenous herbs and plants, promoting awareness and appreciation for the natural world. The sanctuary's commitment to conservation and education has resulted in a positive outcome for the Nilgiri tahr population and a memorable experience for visitors.

articles.title_en: Housing Census in Andhra Pradesh from May 1; self-enumeration to start on April 16
articles.summary_en: The house listing will continue until May 30, with citizens able to submit details online from April 16 to 30. Over 1.08 lakh enumerators have been deployed as the Census goes digital for the first time.
articles.article_en: **Census 2021 Goes Digital: Counting Houses on for May 30 Deadline** The nationwide house listing and housing census has been extended until May 30, according to officials. Following this, online submissions of details will take place from April 16 to 30. For the first time in the country's history, the census has gone digital, with over 1.08 lakh enumerators tasked with collecting data. The enumerators have been equipped with mobile phones and tablets to record information. Residents will be required to submit details of their houses online, providing crucial information such as building type, total floors, and residential area. The decision to go digital aims to increase efficiency and speed, reducing reliance on paperwork and manual data collection. The census is an important exercise aimed at updating the country's population figures and infrastructure needs.

articles.title_en: DISCOMs will buy PM Surya Ghar solar power in A.P.: Energy Minister Gottipati Ravi Kumar
articles.summary_en: The Andhra Pradesh Energy Department will purchase 1,500 MW of solar power from the SPV plant proposed by PM Surya Ghar in Andhra Pradesh.
articles.article_en: Andhra Pradesh Energy Minister Gottipati Ravi Kumar announced that Distribution Companies (DISCOMs) in the state have agreed to purchase power from the proposed 1,210 MW solar power plant, Surya Ghar, to be set up in Andhra Pradesh. The project will cover an area of 3,191 acres and is expected to reduce the state's carbon footprint by 1.9 million tons annually. The plant will be equipped with a combination of monocrystalline and polycrystalline solar panels, aiming for an average annual production of 1,400 MUs. The investment for the project is estimated to be Rs 1,240 crore. The Energy Minister stated that the state aims to become a leading provider of renewable energy, and the Surya Ghar project is a significant step towards achieving that goal. With this project, the state's renewable energy capacity is expected to increase significantly, contributing to a cleaner and greener environment.

articles.title_en: BDA calls tender for Peripheral Ring Road-1 project
articles.summary_en: The peripheral ring road will be 23 km in length, of which only a 19.80 km stretch has been taken up for construction in package-1.
articles.article_en: **Government Greenlights Peripheral Ring Road Project** The long-awaited Peripheral Ring Road project has got the government's seal of approval, with work on the 23-kilometer stretch scheduled to begin soon. Out of the planned length, a significant 19.80 kilometers will be constructed under package-1. The remaining length will be completed in the next phases. This ambitious project aims to ease congestion on the city's main roads and provide a smoother commute for residents. The new ring road is expected to connect various cities and towns on the outskirts of the city, fostering economic growth and development in the region. With the project's commencement, the authorities are optimistic about its potential to transform the city's transportation infrastructure and improve the overall quality of life for its residents. Further details regarding the project's timeline and budget were not disclosed.

articles.title_en: Polavaram Collector mandates field visit for officials
articles.summary_en: Polavaram Collector mandates field visit for officials. The move aims to streamline the functioning of various government departments and ensure efficient delivery of services to the public.
articles.article_en: Polavaram Collector Mandates Field Visit for Officials The Polavaram Collector, S. Satyanarayana, has initiated a mandatory field inspection for officials in the district. As per a recent directive, all officials, including those at the divisional and mandal levels, have been instructed to visit the remote and disadvantaged areas of the district. This move aims to increase transparency and accountability among government officials. The Collector's order comes as part of a larger effort to bridge the gap between the government and rural communities, often neglected in the administration. The official visit is expected to bring officials closer to the ground level and enable them to understand the real needs and issues faced by the local population. This move is seen as a step towards good governance and will likely promote community engagement and development.

articles.title_en: Kerala High Court urges govt. to release DA arrears of employees, teachers
articles.summary_en: The Kerala High Court has directed the government to release pending DA arrears of state government employees and teachers. A contempt petition was filed after the government failed to implement the order, resulting in the court's latest move.
articles.article_en: In a significant development, the Kerala High Court has urged the state government to release the pending Dearness Allowance (DA) arrears of its employees and teachers. The court's direction came in response to a petition filed by the Indian Government Employees Federation of Kerala. The federation had sought relief for the employees who had been waiting for the payment of DA arrears since 2016. The court ruled that the failure of the government to release the arrears was unconstitutional and unreasonable. The court ordered the government to release the arrears immediately, stating that employees should not be made to suffer due to administrative delays. With this order, thousands of employees and teachers in the state are expected to receive their long-pending dues, bringing relief to them and their families.

articles.title_en: DGP appeals to Maoists to surrender and join mainstream
articles.summary_en: The Director General of Police has appealed to Maoists to surrender and join the mainstream, offering a chance for rehabilitation and integration into society.
articles.article_en: Direndra Kumar, the Director General of Police (DGP) of Odisha, has made a heartfelt appeal to the Maoist rebels to surrender and integrate into the mainstream society. In a special press conference, he emphasized the need to reconcile and pledged to provide safe passage and rehabilitation support to those who lay down their arms. The DGP stated that the government is committed to addressing the concerns of the marginalized communities and providing them with a platform to articulate their demands and grievances. He also assured that the security forces would engage in constructive dialogue with the Maoists, focusing on solving social and economic issues rather than resorting to violence. Kumar emphasized that the government's stance is clear: development and progress are the primary goals, and the Maoists can be part of this journey by surrendering and accepting the offer. He implored the rebels to seize this opportunity, stating that the government's doors are open to those willing to lay down their arms and join the mainstream.

articles.title_en: N.K. Premachandran alleges CPI(M)-BJP ‘deal’ in 18 seats, targets CM over SDPI links
articles.summary_en: N.K. Premachandran alleges CPI(M)-BJP deal in 18 seats and targets CM over SDPI links. The Left chief also questions the CM's association with Social Democratic Party of India (SDPI).
articles.article_en: KOLLAM, Kerala - N.K. Premachandran, a senior Congress leader and former union minister, claimed on Tuesday that the CPI(M) and BJP have reached a deal to engage in a friendly contest on the same 18 assembly seats in Kerala. Premachandran, a Lok Sabha member from Adoor, also accused the state CPI(M) chief Pinarayi Vijayan of taking directions from the BJP and of maintaining close links with SDPI. Premachandran's assertion comes amid controversies surrounding the CPI(M)’s consensus on alliances along the way, as both the Left coalition and the UDF are aspiring to retain power. Alleging Vijayan's betrayal of communist ideology in selecting a Left candidate after losing in each election, Premachandran demanded that Kerala Chief Minister be made to answer for this stance.

articles.title_en: IN-SPACe invites applications for student competition
articles.summary_en: The national level finales of both competitions will take place between October and November 2026 in Kushinagar, Uttar Pradesh. All events are expected to congregate in this northern state as per the latest auspices.
articles.article_en: **Major Cultural Events to Convene in Uttar Pradesh in 2026** Kushinagar, Uttar Pradesh - The Government of India has announced that the national level finales for two prestigious cultural competitions will be held in Kushinagar, Uttar Pradesh, between October and November 2026. The events, which are expected to attract participants from across the country, will showcase the rich cultural diversity of India. The competitions, details of which are yet to be revealed, will bring together talented individuals and teams from various regions of the country to compete in various aspects of culture, art, and heritage. The government has made elaborate arrangements to ensure the smooth conduct of the events, which are expected to be a major cultural spectacle. The city of Kushinagar, known for its historical significance and rich cultural heritage, is all set to host these significant events, showcasing its capabilities as a premier cultural destination in the country.

articles.title_en: Andhra Pradesh Lokayukta recovers ₹15.29 crore, disposes of 3,654 complaints in 2025
articles.summary_en: The office of Lokayukta remained vacant throughout the year. Upa-Lokayukta P. Rajani performed duties instead, authorized by the Andhra Pradesh Lokayukta Act, 1983.
articles.article_en: As per the annual report, the office of the Lokayukta remained vacant, with Upa-Lokayukta P. Rajani discharging all responsibilities under the provisions of the Andhra Pradesh Lokayukta Act, 1983.
